summary: Unable to create temporary file with FileAccess.create_temp() without specifying a prefix

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M1 - Apple M1 (8 threads)

### Issue description

making a temporary directory with just one argument results in this error
```gdscript
var temp_file = FileAccess.create_temp(FileAccess.READ_WRITE)
var temp_file = FileAccess.create_temp(FileAccess.READ_WRITE, "") # or this
var temp_file = FileAccess.create_temp(FileAccess.READ_WRITE, "   ") # or this

#   ERROR: Error while creating temporary file: "" is not a valid prefix.
```

the resulting `temp_file` is empty

---

using two argument also results in an error
```gdscript
var temp_file = FileAccess.create_temp(FileAccess.READ_WRITE, "example")

# ERROR: Error while creating temporary file: "" is not a valid extension.
```

someone in discord says

```cpp
Ref<FileAccess> FileAccess::create_temp(int p_mode_flags, const String &p_prefix, const String &p_extension, bool p_keep, Error *r_error) {
    const String ERROR_COMMON_PREFIX = "Error while creating temporary file";

    if (!p_prefix.is_valid_filename()) {
        *r_error = ERR_FILE_BAD_PATH;
        ERR_FAIL_V_MSG(Ref<FileAccess>(), vformat(R"(%s: "%s" is not a valid prefix.)", ERROR_COMMON_PREFIX, p_prefix));
    }
```

> is_valid_filename fails for empty strings, but the default value is empty

### Steps to reproduce

- In any project, create a new script (gdscript)
- write this
```
@tool
extends EditorScript

func _run() -> void:
	var temp_file = FileAccess.create_temp(FileAccess.READ_WRITE)
	print("succeed")
```
- File > Run the script
- Notice the error

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Typed arrays not updating when keyframed in AnimationPlayer

description.text:

3. Add AnimationPlayer to scene as child of original Node

5. Assign setter function to typed array and integer that prints values of said properties
6. Set animation to play on startup and loop
7. Play scene in editor and confirm that both properties are updating in the debug display
8. Export project (with debug) with the node/animation player as the main scene and run exported build

summary: macOS: Embedded window support.

description.text:
Embedded window support for macOS.

This feature is implemented using `CARemoteLayer`, which is similar to how we implemented it for OpenEmu. This works by creating a CALayer in the child process and passing the CAContextID back to the Godot Editor. The editor is then able to create an instance of `CARemoteLayer` and display the rendered output in real-time, as if it was hosted in the app. A major difference is that we serialise the events from the host app, the Godot Editor, and process them in the running game.

The following additional state / events are processed correctly:

- [x] Joystick add / remove events must be sent to the child process

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/43aa5846-1565-46ad-8fb7-bbf2315ba638

- [x] Joystick rumble timestamps (can't demonstrate that in a video)
- DisplayDriver events
  - [x] cursor changes within embedded window

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb1cc98f-4259-4cc9-ba92-4ff2210e74f5

  - [x] IME events

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58f6d80f-f19a-42ca-94aa-eb6761ce3a43

- [x] OpenGL Support, selection, panning, and more

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1ae09395-50c6-4263-8e3b-8435553face3

- [x] Mouse Capture and `⌥+ESC` shortcut, always available to the editor, to release capture

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f120c280-3758-438f-b42b-505708ea3a1a


___

- *Production edit: This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11453.*

summary: Tool scripts with await ready in export variable setter functions use the default value in exported projects

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot 4.5.dev3 and Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600G with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

With a tool script, adding 
```gdscript
if not is_node_ready():
	await ready
```
to an export variables setter function before setting the variable's value will cause that variable to only be set in editor.

For example, the following code:
```gdscript
@export var variable_1 : String = "Default Value" : 
	set(value):
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		
		variable_1 = value
```
won't immediately run during export. This causes the variable to always use the default value, rather than an overridden one in a scene. **This only occurs with tool scripts. Regular scripts are unaffected.**

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a custom scene and give it a **tool script** with a variable with a setter function with an ``await ready`` inside it like so:
```gdscript
@export var variable_1 : String = "Default Value" : 
	set(value):
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		
		variable_1 = value
```
2. Add that scene to another, and override the default value.
3. Have some way of viewing that value in both editor and export. I used a button with a print function connected to ``pressed``
4. You will notice that in editor, the overridden value is normal. But in export the value is not.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The issue is also visible in brand new projects, but here is one already setup with buttons to print values:
[export-variable-export-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19948351/export-variable-export-bug.zip)

summary: When a previously saved scene is opened in the editor, when child of a CanvasLayer, control nodes with "Custom" Anchors Preset and nonzero anchor offsets show the incorrect value when the anchor points values match a preset

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When a previously saved scene is opened in the editor, when child of a CanvasLayer, control nodes with "Custom" Anchors Preset and nonzero anchor offsets show the incorrect value when the anchor points values match a preset. Since the anchor offsets are nonzero, it should not be considered to match a an anchors preset value, and should be considered "Custom".

Note that this does not occur if the Anchor Points are different from an Anchors Preset's anchor points, in that case it correctly shows "Custom" as the Anchors Preset value when the saved scene is opened in the editor.

This bug does not seem to affect the actual positioning of the control node, though it does affect the value of `anchors_preset` in the `.tscn` file.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new scene with CanvasLayer as the root node.
2. Add a Button as a child of the Canvas Layer
3. Choose "Custom" for Anchors Preset.
4. Set Anchor Point (Left 0.5, Top, 0.0, Right 0.5, Bottom 0.0), set Anchor Offsets (Top 100px).
5. Save the scene
6. Select the "Button" node in the inspector, if not already selected.
    - It should look like this screenshot, with "Custom" for Anchors Preset.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aa8d7bcb-ac3f-4151-bf6e-04cad73bc8b7)
7. Close the scene
8. Open the scene again
9. Click the "Button" node.
11. Check the Anchors Preset. Note that Anchors Preset incorrectly says "Center Top" (instead of Custom)
    - It will (incorrectly) look like this screenshot
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7417e34-366f-4321-b44a-971a749dd791)
12. Also note that the node will be in the correct place (100px below the actual center top location)
13. Also note that if you choose "Custom" for Anchors Preset again, the Anchor Offsets will be displayed and will be correct.


You may use the MRP below, however due to the nature of the bug, when loaded it will be as if starting from step 8 above. You may use the editor to change the "Button" node Anchors Preset value to "Custom" and save, then close and reopen the scene to reproduce the bug from the MRP.

summary: Duplicating scene with editable children caused duplicated exported node arrays to reference the original copy

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.mono.official [4c311cbee], v4.5.dev3.mono.official [55fc6e73f], v4.3.stable.mono.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable.mono - Windows 10.0.26100 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

This issue only occurs in C#.

When duplicating a scene node with editable children that exports an array containing references to its children, the duplicated node's array contains references to the original node's children instead of its own.

I would expect the duplicated node's array to contain references to its own children as this is the behavior observed with non-array exported properties that reference the duplicated node's children.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene
2. Give the scene root node a C# script exporting a Godot array of Nodes
3. Add a child node to the scene and add a reference to it to the exported array
4. Create another scene and add the original scene as a child
5. Enable editable children on the child scene
6. Duplicate the child scene
7. Mouse over the exported array on the duplicated scene to see the paths of its elements still reference the children of the original scene
### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ArrayExportTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19933175/ArrayExportTest.zip)

To Reproduce using the MRP:
1. Build the C# project
2. Open Scene.tscn
3. Duplicate SubSceneGDScript
4. Note that the duplicated node's array properly references the duplicate's children
5. Duplicate SubSceneC#

summary: [Web] Fix cut-copy-paste

description.text:
Believe it or not, copy-cut-paste on the Web platform seems to be completely broken. (I mean cut/copy/paste from outside to the game or from the game to outside.) [Try it out.](https://editor.godotengine.org/) This PR fixes this. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9ce8412-9105-42e6-98c8-63151b66db88

The issue is caused by the fact that we use `event.preventDefault()` for every key input. This makes it impossible for the browser to actually trigger and dispatch the `ClipboardEvent` needed to read the browser's clipboard. It is only available on [transient user activation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/User_activation) due to [security considerations](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Clipboard_API#security_considerations).

So, I've added exceptions for {Ctrl,Cmd}-{X,C,V} (ctrl/cmd depends on the host). Keyboard-based copy/cut/paste aren't prevented by default, and this fixes the issues with copy/pasting.

So, it will be possible to copy/paste code from and to the browser. Very useful for code editors.

summary: Game using `canvas_items` stretch mode crashes when window size is changed

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.dev3.mono.official [55fc6e73f]

Not reproducible in v4.5.dev2.mono.official

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3570K CPU @ 3.40GHz (4 threads)

Another system where the bug occurs:
Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

My game uses `canvas_items` stretch mode with following parameters:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69ebd1b2-ed80-4256-92ca-7e190ac0a2a0)

When I resize the game window, either going to fullscreen mode or dragging the window border, the game crashes when the scaling should change, for example from 2 to 3 (when integer scaling is used).

What the crash looks like: game stops responding, Windows' busy mouse cursor (arrow with a circle) appears, and finally the game window just disappears. 

No error messages or anything is displayed, not even with exported game with `--verbose`.

When running the game inside Godot editor, crash happens about 90% of the time. When running exported game, crash probability is a bit lower, maybe 50%. If the game survives the first window resize without crashing, the game doesn't crash no matter how many times the window is resized. Crash happens always with the first window resize.

It doesn't matter which scaling mode `integer` or `fractional` is used, both crash.

If I change stretch mode to `viewport`, game doesn't crash anymore.

I found out that the theme in the reproduction project's main window has something to do with the crash. If the theme is removed, crash wont happen anymore.

### Steps to reproduce

See section Minimal reproduction project.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[crash-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19937973/crash-test.zip)

Run the game as non-embedded so that the window resizing is easier.

1. Start the game
2. Resize window as much as you want, no crash
3. Click the Start button
4. Resize window smaller so that the integer scaling will kick in. **--> crash**

"Fix": Main window has a theme, `main_theme.tres`. Clear that theme from the main window. Now the crash wont happen anymore.

summary: [.NET] Android app crashes on startup when DLL file is missing from cache

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: `4.4.1.stable` and the version with the preload hook applied from PR #105262 (Godot_v4.5-android-no-copy_mono_export_templates.tpz)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.4.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads)

### Issue description

**Related**: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104721#issuecomment-2833357837

**Problem:**  
On the Android platform, the OS or cleaner apps can delete files in the cache directory at any time without warning ([reference](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75628346/can-files-in-the-cache-directory-be-deleted-at-any-time)). Thus, storing critical runtime files such as DLLs only in the cache directory is unsafe. If a required DLL file is deleted by OS, the game crashes immediately at startup.  
Currently, even after applying the patch from PR #105262, the app still loads DLL files from the cache directory, leaving it vulnerable to this issue.

**Suggested Changes:**  
- I believe that PR #105262 should be revised so that it loads DLL files directly from the PCK instead of relying on cached files.  
- Alternatively, a mechanism should be introduced to check whether the cached file exists and whether its size matches the expected value. If there is a mismatch, the file should be recopied from the PCK.

---

**Details:**  
After releasing the game on Android, we started receiving reports that the game would occasionally fail to launch.  
We updated the game using a custom build that included the patches from issue #104721 and PR #105262.  
After applying these patches, crash reports decreased but did not fully disappear, and we continued to receive bug reports indicating crashes with the following error:
```
* Assertion at /__w/1/s/src/mono/mono/metadata/assembly.c:2718, condition 'corlib' not met
```

To investigate, I stress-tested the app by repeatedly launching and closing it, but the problem did not reoccur under normal use.  
I then deleted the `System.Private.CoreLib.dll` file from the cache directory (`/data/data/com.example.newgameproject/cache/data_New Game Project_android_arm64/System.Private.CoreLib.dll`) via Android Studio.

After this, the app immediately crashed at startup with the same assertion failure:  
```
.NET: Loading assembly 'System.Private.CoreLib.dll' from '/data/data/com.example.newgameproject/cache/data_New Game Project_android_arm64/System.Private.CoreLib.dll'.
* Assertion at /__w/1/s/src/mono/mono/metadata/assembly.c:2718, condition 'corlib' not met
```

This confirms that even after applying the patch from PR #105262, the app still attempts to load DLL files from the cache directory.  
The preload hook does not fully prevent the app from depending on cached DLL files, and thus the issue remains reproducible.

Additionally, considering that Android OS can delete cache files at any time, relying on cached DLLs poses a serious stability risk for production applications.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new empty Node2D scene.
2. Extract `Godot_v4.5-android-no-copy_mono_export_templates.tpz` as a `.zip` file, then extract the `android_source.zip` inside it into the project's `android/build` directory.
3. Create a `.gdignore` file inside the `android/build` directory.
4. Create a `.build_version` file in the `android` directory and add `4.4.1.stable.mono` as its content.
5. In the editor, go to **Project > Tools > C# > Create C# Solutions**.
6. Go to **Project > Export > Add > Android**, enable **Use Gradle Build**, then close the dialog.
7. Enable **Verbose stdout** under **Project > Debug > Settings > stdout**.
8. Deploy remotely by selecting your Android device.
9. Observe the following logs:
    ```
    Godot Engine v4.5.android-no-copy.mono.custom_build.3c3fb8e76 - https://godotengine.org
    ...
    .NET: Initializing module...
    Found monosgen: libmonosgen-2.0.so
    .NET: CoreCLR initialized
    .NET: Loading assembly 'System.Private.CoreLib.dll' from '/data/data/com.example.newgameproject/cache/data_New Game Project_android_arm64/System.Private.CoreLib.dll'.
    ```

summary: Mark referenced packages in SDK as implicitly defined

description.text:
Add `IsImplicitlyDefined="true"` to `PackageReference` tags in Sdk.targets.

Will fix two problems i had with C# scripting.

## 1. Prevent Package Manager in Visual Studio from trying to update SDK packages

When viewing installed packages in IDE, Package Manager suggests updating `Godot.SourceGenerators`, `GodotSharp` and `GodotSharpEditor` to the latest version. Looks like this action doesn't do anything, but it can mislead user into thinking they upgraded their environment to the latest version.

Adding `IsImplicitlyDefined` should prevent IDE from changing versions of these packages.

<details>

<summary>Screenshots</summary>

Before:

![vs-packagemanager-bugged](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8fb2823a-b6d2-4739-ac1f-bcaf05f6f123)

After:

![vs-packagemanager-fixed](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/221e1a31-6985-401c-b516-eb20bdce05a0)

</details> 

## 2. Restore in Godot project fails when Central Package Management enabled

When using CPM with Godot, restore fails with error:

```
/path/cpm-test/CPM Test.csproj : error NU1008: Projects that use central package version management should not define the version on the PackageReference items but on the PackageVersion items: Godot.SourceGenerators;GodotSharp;GodotSharpEditor.
```

[Example repository](https://github.com/hexog/godot-cpm-error)

With `IsImplicitlyDefined="true"`, NuGet will not try to manage the SDK packages, resolving this error.

---

This change is backwards compatible and should not break anything in existing projects. However, since `IsImplicitlyDefined` is not properly documented, I’d appreciate it if someone more knowledgeable in MSBuild could ensure that nothing will break unexpectedly.

What i found about `IsImplicitlyDefined`:

https://github.com/NuGet/Home/issues/13529
https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/pull/43151/files#r1746148520

summary: Fix item translation and icon in the Anchors Preset dropdown

description.text:
## Problem

I noticed that the last two items in `anchors_preset` property's dropdown menu were not translated. Also, all the icons in this menu does not react to light/dark theme switch.

![The last two presets were not translated](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/40f44d92-c26d-4bb9-a801-a6c287da9142)

## Cause

The preset names are actually not extracted from the `anchors_preset` property's hint string, but from texts in the control layout toolbar.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e37c6261ea48b1a339c469282df7e9b7c073a72f/editor/plugins/control_editor_plugin.cpp#L651-L655

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e37c6261ea48b1a339c469282df7e9b7c073a72f/scene/gui/control.cpp#L3990-L3993

It's `VCenter Wide` in the toolbar, while it's `V Center Wide` after prefix-trimming and calling `capitalize()` on `PresetVCenterWide`. So this entry does not have translation available.

The reason for generating names by using string magic seems to be to make it easier to concatenate the icon name (e.g. `ControlAlignVCenterWide`). I believe it's kind of over-engineering :P

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e37c6261ea48b1a339c469282df7e9b7c073a72f/editor/plugins/control_editor_plugin.cpp#L194-L199

## Solution

This PR updates the menu item's icon based on its enum value when editor's theme changes.

Once this is done, the string magic around hint string is no longer necessary. The preset names can be marked for translation normally.

p.s. Also cleaned up header includes in the files I touched.

_Update:_ Marked StringNames in the static array as static so that the leak checker will be happy :P

summary: Walk up inheritance hierarchy when finding which script's docs to open

description.text:
When opening a Node's documentation, the Scene Tree dock currently checks the node's script to see if it is a global class (eg. via `class_name`).
This allows convenient access to custom node types' documentation.

However, the current implementation does not account for inheritance—

If opening docs for a non-global script that inherits a custom global class, the dock will still default to using the Node's native class instead of the custom class.

This PR makes the method walk up the script's inheritance hierarchy to find the most specific global class, including custom types if present.

summary: CustomCallables keep a references to an old free_func on hotreload

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4 dafc4739

### System information

linux

### Issue description

When creating a custom callable, and connecting it to a node, if the extension from which the callable was create gets reloaded (hotreload), the editor may/will crash.

I tracked it down to the `free_func` called [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/dafc47390c39a6be75cf1705f527ccc1b38a32b0/core/extension/gdextension_interface.cpp#L234) _(but i guess any other `ffi_func` would do the trick)_
i've added `WARN_PRINT` around it (on a local copy of this commit) and it clearly crashes calling it
```cpp
	~CallableCustomExtension() {
		WARN_PRINT("before free");
		if (free_func != nullptr) {
			free_func(userdata);
		}
		WARN_PRINT("after free");
	}
```
I get this output on the terminal (trying to reload the project after recompiling)
```bash
Building for architecture x86_64 on platform linux
scons: done reading SConscript files.
scons: Building targets ...
scons: `godot-cpp/bin/libgodot-cpp.linux.template_debug.x86_64.a' is up to date.
Compiling shared src/example.cpp ...
Linking Shared Library demo/bin/libgdexample.linux.template_debug.x86_64.so ...
scons: done building targets.
WARNING: before free
     at: ~CallableCustomExtension (core/extension/gdextension_interface.cpp:233)
*** stack smashing detected ***: terminated
```

the problematic lines on the example (which is one of the [tests from godot-cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-cpp/blob/master/test/src/example.cpp) with some light tweaking) is [this one](https://github.com/Ventilador/callable-crash/blob/0fb623112e508ebb14519e4a35aa05160b2cc796/src/example.cpp#L25) (having a destructor)

and [this one](https://github.com/Ventilador/callable-crash/blob/0fb623112e508ebb14519e4a35aa05160b2cc796/src/example.cpp#L117) (registering the callable on `this`)

it may be related to [this gdext-rust ticket](https://github.com/godot-rust/gdext/issues/984) or at least it's what it took me to figure out the crashes

### Steps to reproduce

1) Create a custom callable using `callable_custom_create2`
2) Rebuild your extension
3) trigger an editor refresh (with focus so it reloads the extension)
4) either Quit to Project List or Reload Project _(sometimes even changing scene tabs, i think when they where never loaded but the extension was)_


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/Ventilador/callable-crash

summary: Remove `alt` key transforms in 3D to prevent navigation key conflicts

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

See: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/87756#issuecomment-2831964966

Removes the ability to use the `alt` key to initiate transforms outside the geometry of selected nodes. 

Added code to handle similar functionality but only when initiating inside geometry of selected nodes.

The alt key is often used for navigating in 3D in different ways depending on the scheme. Godot offers a lot of flexibility in this regard, so there is high chance for conflict. Instead of trying to hard-code specific scenarios I think it makes sense to just remove this functionality entirely and recommended users to use blender-style shortcuts. This allows for a fully customizable solution. 

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eafee8c2-2f76-42b6-8a43-dfd4a1acd2f3)

summary: Never show progress bar when moving files to trash on Windows to prevent losing focus

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/99552. This also affects the Godot editor itself when deleting files on Windows.

Hides all UI when sending files to the recycle bin on Windows. With the previous flags, we were still requesting a progress bar to be displayed. It typically wouldn't be visible because trashing even large files is often nearly instant (though I could see it when deleting a large directory with many files), but even when not visibly appearing the progress dialog would still momentarily steal focus from the main window.

See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/shellapi/ns-shellapi-shfileopstructw#fof_no_ui

Based on the implementations for other platforms, I'm assuming that showing a system progress bar is not needed or desired, so changing this in order to avoid the focus steal should be okay.

summary: Camera2D ignores rotation pivot on small limits, even if there's enough room for it to rotate.

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

Windows 10, Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

Issue is pretty self descriptive, setting any camera's limits to values that totals a size too close to the window size messes up the rotation

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23a02e71-b163-475e-99bb-4f611c96c280

summary: Control's global position set to wrong value

description.text:
### Tested versions

Works in 4.5 dev2, broken in dev3
Regression from #105482
CC @maxpiepenbrink 

### System information

W10

### Issue description

I have a complex menu layout that involves a control used to highlight options. My code to set its position no longer works in dev3.

Correct behavior (dev2):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/481672b1-b3ff-4021-b13e-28b2bfc81d6c

Broken (dev3)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/259c4450-1eeb-4680-ab29-6907c8d59e7c

The code that moves the highlight rect looks more or less like this:
```GDScript
menu_selector.custom_minimum_size.x = width
menu_selector.set_anchors_and_offsets_preset(Control.PRESET_CENTER)
menu_selector.global_position.y = option.global_position.y - (menu_selector.size.y - option.size.y) * 0.5 + (4 if main_menu else 0)
```
I added a print for the values in the last line. The right side of the equation is the same between versions, but the final global value is incorrect. Important bit seems to be that the highlight rect is set to centered anchor preset _in the editor_. With top-left preset it's not working correctly in both engine versions (even though I force the preset in code).

btw, while it's a regression from #105482, that's a whole chain of PRs: #93908 -> #104451 -> #105482

### Steps to reproduce

Run the MRP and press up/down to change options.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Stripped down version of the menu seen above.

[Globalbug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19917486/Globalbug.zip)

The panel is moved in `update_selector()` method in Options script.

summary: Performance drop when moving objects in range of shadow enabled lights

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.stable, 4.4.1, 4.5.dev1, 4.5.dev2, 4.3.stable, 4.2.2.stable, 4.0.4.stable, 4.0.stable
- Not Reproducible in 4.3.stable, 4.3.rc2, 4.3.dev3

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT (RADV NAVI10) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

When moving, or hiding then showing, objects inside the range of a OmniLight3D or Spotlight3D with shadows enabled and while the camera is watching, the performance tanks, seemingly because of an influx of draw calls. These extra draw calls seem to be called every frame until the objects leave camera view. I expect that hiding and showing a node should not have a chronic side effect.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70ecdcfa-ee3b-4582-b59f-61d8fe9bb0e1

This occurs in the editor as well.

The reason i marked the 4.3 versions as not reproducible is because it always seem to call those extra draw calls, regardless if you moved anything beforehand. All other Godot versions i tested exhibited the bugged behaviour.



### Steps to reproduce

The bug occurs when moving objects or lights and both when hiding and showing them individually and in groups. The light node settings are set in node_3d.gd

 1. Open the project.
 2. Run the scene.
 3. Hide then show the "Boxes" node in either remote or local node tree.

description.text:

summary: Changin wind source rotation for Area3D doesn't affect SoftBody3D object

### Issue description

I have a SoftBody3D and an Area3D with wind parameter that vary with keyboard input:

summary: Warn when running low on disk space when performing certain editor operations

description.text:
- Follow-up to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/62418.

The following editor operations will now warn the user if the available space on the target disk is low:

- Saving a scene (< 1 GiB).
- Importing a set of resources (< 2 GiB).
- Downloading export templates (< 4 GiB).
- Installing export templates (< 4 GiB).
- Exporting a project (< 10 GiB).
- Packing a project as a ZIP file (< 10 GiB).

___

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/95111.
- See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/45354
and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105742.

summary: LSP connection with external IDE updates only when opening Editor Window

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible in 4.4. .NET stable for Windows and Linux
-Not reproducible in 4.3 .NET stable for Windows

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Intel(R) UHD Graphics (Intel Corporation; 27.20.100.9316) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10885H CPU @ 2.40GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using an external IDE like Rider or Visual Studio Code (both reproduced the issue) and connecting to the Godot Editor for the LSP functionality, the connection is not constant. If you make a change in a .gd file, that would lead to a warning or an error, those will not occur until you go to the opened Godot Editor window and come back, as if the changes are not recognised until this happens. 

### Steps to reproduce

The issue also reproduces for VS code, but these steps will be for working with JetBrains Rider.
### Steps:
1. Open the Godot project in Rider 
2. Open the same project in the Godot Engine
3. Ensure the LSP connection between the IDE and the engine is established. 
4. Create a file, e.g., first.gd in Rider via `Add | File`
first.gd:
```gdscript
class_name First extends Resource

enum FirstEnum{
val1, val2, val3}
```
5. Create another file, e.g second.gd, also via `Add | File`
6. Try using the class defined in first.gd :
```gdscript
var test1 = First.FirstEnum.val1
```
### Expected result:
Expected to see completion for the defined class. Expected completion and code analysis results to be up to date without manually switching to Godot editor. If you change the class name in the first.gd, a warning or an error should appear for the second.gd.
### Actual result:
No completion appears until `Alt+Tab` to Godot and back to Rider and then perform any action with editor in Rider (for ex, type space). The warnings and errors also will be displayed only after opening the Godot Engine window and coming back to Rider.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Cannot switch from fullscreen to windowed mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.2.2.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 (nvidia; 550.120) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Switching window mode from fullscreen to windowed does not work in certain circumstances:
- Game must be launched in fullscreen mode. When it starts in windowed mode switching to fullscreen and back works without issues.
- It must be launched on the secondary monitor (1920x1080). If that monitor is set as primary or if I disable the other monitor (1920x1200) everything works fine.

Main suspect is the Nvidia driver. My other games, that I developed on the same computer using Godot 4.2 and 4.3 before I bought the Nvidia card, also have this issue now. But it may also be that I just never noticed it before because I rarely launch projects in fullscreen during development and even less so on the secondary monitor.

### Steps to reproduce

Using this code, I can see in the log that the window mode changes briefly to 0 (windowed) when I press the 'toggle_fullscreen' action but than changes back to 3 (fullscreen) after just few frames:

```gdscript
func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	print(DisplayServer.window_get_mode())
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("toggle_fullscreen"):
		if DisplayServer.window_get_mode() == DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN:
			DisplayServer.window_set_mode(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED)
		else:
			DisplayServer.window_set_mode(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Launch the project and press 'F' to toggle between fullscreen and windowed modes. Make sure that the project is not embedded in the editor and that it starts in fullscreen on the secondary monitor.
[fullscreen_issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19907887/fullscreen_issue.zip)
